title: spirabutilon citrinum
intro: Spirabutilon citrinum, also known as the Yellow Bells plant, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its antispasmodic and expectorant properties. Spirabutilon citrinum has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving anxiety and stress, and acting as an antiseptic to combat infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, and can also be used to make topical creams and ointments to soothe skin irritations.
